headline: Bank Profits Hit RM42B Record While Lending Margins Squeeze SMEs
context: Malaysia's 8 listed banks reported combined net profit of RM42.3 billion in 2025, driven by fee income and treasury gains, even as SMEs complained of tightening credit conditions and higher effective borrowing costs.

cards.big: RM42.3 billion in bank profits — record earnings while 52% of SME loan applications get rejected
cards.sub: Banks earned RM18 billion from fees and charges alone — a 34% increase driven by digital transaction charges

cards.big: Average SME effective borrowing rate at 7.8% versus large corporation rate of 4.2% — 3.6 percentage point penalty for being small
cards.sub: Banks allocate only 18% of lending to SMEs despite BNM's 22% target — preferring safer corporate and mortgage lending

cards.big: Bank CEO pay averaged RM8.4 million in 2025 while median bank employee earned RM4,200 monthly — ratio of 167:1
cards.sub: Banking sector employs 158,000 workers but 28,000 positions eliminated through digitalisation since 2020

cards.big: BNM's moral suasion approach to SME lending targets has failed for 6 consecutive years with no regulatory consequence
cards.sub: Competition Commission has never investigated potential oligopolistic pricing in Malaysia's concentrated banking sector

cards.big: When banks earn RM42 billion while small businesses can't get loans, the financial system serves itself — not the economy
Should BNM convert SME lending targets from voluntary guidelines to binding requirements with licence renewal conditions?
